wolf_cull.name: Cull the Gaunt Wolves
wolf_cull.flavor: The wolves at the wood-edge have learned the taste of people. Thin them.

scavver_purge.name: Purge the Scavvers
scavver_purge.flavor: They are crawling closer to the village fire each night.

bandit_hunt.name: Bring the Cutthroats to Heel
bandit_hunt.flavor: Three farms have gone silent this month.

drowned_thresher_quiet.name: Lay the Drowned Threshers to Rest
drowned_thresher_quiet.flavor: They still reap. Stop them and the Reach can be sown again.

magistrate_unmade.name: Unseat the Pall-Sworn Magistrates
magistrate_unmade.flavor: Mourncross will not be itself while they keep passing sentence.

warden_hunt.name: Hunt the Stone Sentinels
warden_hunt.flavor: They guard tombs whose names have worn off. Free them.

dire_wolf_cull.name: Cull the Dire Wolves at the Wood-Edge
dire_wolf_cull.flavor: A larger pack moves at the wood-edge — the bigger, slower wolves the woodsmen used to thin in winter. Thin them again.

road_to_the_reach.name: The Road to the Reach Is Not Safe
road_to_the_reach.flavor: Four farms along the Witherwood-Sodden road have gone silent. The cutthroats have decided about the road.

silent_at_dusk.name: Silent at Dusk
silent_at_dusk.flavor: The scavvers come closer each dusk. Six of them, and the village fire can burn one count longer tonight.

pelts_for_the_tanner.name: Pelts for the Tanner
pelts_for_the_tanner.flavor: Old Halna's daughter, the tanner, asks for twelve. She will pay in coin and in something her grandmother kept the recipe of.

teeth_for_the_smith.name: Teeth for the Smith's Keepsake
teeth_for_the_smith.flavor: The smith strings them on wire for a small charm against the Pall. Ten teeth, no fewer.

bat_wings_for_the_apothecary.name: Carrion Wings for the Old Apothecary
bat_wings_for_the_apothecary.flavor: She grinds them into the kingdom's old powder for the long nights. Six wings. She will not say what for.

brother_in_the_wood_a.name: The Brother's Last Walk — Find What Took Him
brother_in_the_wood_a.flavor: Tessa's brother walked into the Witherwood three winters ago and did not come back. Find the pack that did it. Start with four.

brother_in_the_wood_b.name: The Brother's Last Walk — Find the Lead Pair
brother_in_the_wood_b.flavor: The pack has a lead pair — the older two, the ones who chose the trail. Bring them down. Then the pack will scatter.

brother_in_the_wood_c.name: The Brother's Last Walk — Bury What Is Left
brother_in_the_wood_c.reward_chronicle_line: They walked the Witherwood for Tessa's brother and brought back what could be brought.
brother_in_the_wood_c.flavor: The pack lead is the last. After it, you can lay what is left in the wood to rest properly. Tessa will know.

read_the_witherwood_stone.name: What the Witherwood Stone Names
read_the_witherwood_stone.flavor: Piranesi's note names two of the older wolves by the colour of their pelts. The stone knew them. Now you do too.

halnas_stag_haunt.name: What Halna's Stag Knew
halnas_stag_haunt.flavor: Old Halna's stag-painting on the lodge wall marks the trail. One wolf walks that trail tonight. Take it. Halna will know.

clean_one_round_wolf.name: A Wolf in a Clean Stroke
clean_one_round_wolf.flavor: Three wolves, three single rounds. The kingdom's old hunters did it this way to spare the pack the noise of the dying.

no_stun_through_the_wood.name: Walk the Wood with a Clear Head
no_stun_through_the_wood.flavor: Take two wolves without letting either of them stagger you. Your footing will steadier itself for the keeping.

protect_the_sworn.name: Bring Your Sworn Back Whole
protect_the_sworn.flavor: Three cutthroats, and your sworn returns at your side after every one. Hirelings remember who keeps them whole.

quiet_hunters_blessing.name: The Quiet Hunter's Blessing
quiet_hunters_blessing.flavor: Only a hunter knows the four particular wolves the wood asks the quiet ones to take. Old Halna will tell a Ranger which.

bless_the_forest_dead.name: Bless the Forest Dead
bless_the_forest_dead.reward_chronicle_line: They walked the Witherwood and blessed the small dead the wood had forgotten to bless.
bless_the_forest_dead.flavor: The carrion bats die at the wood's edge unmarked. Five quiet blessings — the kind only a Cleric carries.

after_the_first_grey.name: After the First Grey
after_the_first_grey.reward_chronicle_line: After their first grey hair, they took a single wolf in the Witherwood, and the wolf went down kindly.
after_the_first_grey.flavor: A particular old wolf has been waiting for a particular kind of climber — one who has been marked by the kingdom's grey. One wolf. One climber. One quiet kill.

find_what_you_left_in_the_woods.name: Find What You Left in the Woods
find_what_you_left_in_the_woods.reward_chronicle_line: They walked the Witherwood for a thing a previous one of them had left behind, and they brought it back.
find_what_you_left_in_the_woods.flavor: A previous you walked here, fell here, left something here. One wolf carries it. Take the wolf. Take the thing. The Chronicle will note both.

thresher_quiet_reach.name: Quiet the Drowned Threshers
thresher_quiet_reach.flavor: The threshers stand in the silted fields and still try to thresh. Five. The reach's old farmers will know which five.

silt_drowners_three.name: Three Silt-Drowners in the Eel-Pools
silt_drowners_three.flavor: They drown the eels first, the water second, the comers third. Three of the drowners. The eel-pools will breathe again.

bog_lurchers_at_low_water.name: Bog-Lurchers at Low Water
bog_lurchers_at_low_water.flavor: When the sluice opens, four of the lurchers crawl up the gravel. Old Halna's daughter knows their count.

thresher_reeds_for_the_weaver.name: Thresher-Reeds for the Weaver
thresher_reeds_for_the_weaver.flavor: The reach's old weaver took only the dead reeds the threshers grew through. Eight, and she will weave the kingdom a sash.

reeds_to_dam_the_breach.name: Reeds to Dam the Breach
reeds_to_dam_the_breach.flavor: The breach in the weir grows by the season. Fifteen thresher-reeds will hold it for another winter. No fewer.

engineer_a_read_the_plans.name: The Engineer's Field — Read His Plans
engineer_a_read_the_plans.flavor: His engineering plans name a single drowner that walks where the main sluice should be. Take it. The Reach will remember.

engineer_b_find_the_weir.name: The Engineer's Field — Find the Broken Weir
engineer_b_find_the_weir.flavor: The threshers stand thickest where the weir broke. Three of them, and the path to the keeper is open.

engineer_c_the_reaper.name: The Engineer's Field — Confront the Keeper
engineer_c_the_reaper.flavor: The Last Reaper kept the weir shut so the kingdom would starve. Find him at the broken sluice. End the keeping.

engineer_d_let_the_field_breathe.name: The Engineer's Field — Let It Breathe
engineer_d_let_the_field_breathe.reward_chronicle_line: They opened the weir the Reaper had shut, and the silt-fields of Hólvar drew their first breath in a century.
engineer_d_let_the_field_breathe.flavor: Two threshers stand at the new flow. After them, the field drains, and Hólvar Reach is one less drowning.

promise_the_field_a_grain.name: Promise the Field a Grain
promise_the_field_a_grain.flavor: The reach's last farmer asks for a grain to be promised — even one. Three threshers, and she will plant. Once you promise, you cannot un-promise.

refuse_the_field_a_grain.name: Refuse the Field a Grain
refuse_the_field_a_grain.flavor: Old Halna's daughter says: do not promise. The Pall hears promises. Three threshers, take the gold, walk on.

hidden_the_reach_remembers.name: The Reach Remembers You
hidden_the_reach_remembers.flavor: The first time you set foot in the silt, one drowner turned its head toward you. It has been waiting since.

hidden_after_almost_drowning.name: After You Almost Drowned
hidden_after_almost_drowning.flavor: The reach almost took you. Two threshers stood between you and the silt that wanted you. Repay them.

thresher_no_damage.name: Untouched Through the Threshing-Field
thresher_no_damage.flavor: Two threshers, and you finish each one without a scratch on you. The reach's old farmers will whisper about a climber who walked clean.

one_round_silt_drowner.name: A Drowner in a Single Round
one_round_silt_drowner.flavor: Two silt-drowners, and each one gone before the silt-song finishes its first verse. One round. No more.

reach_both_steps_marked.name: The Reach Has Marked Both of Your Steps
reach_both_steps_marked.reward_chronicle_line: The Reach had marked their step and their face both, and they took the Reaper anyway.
reach_both_steps_marked.flavor: When the reach has marked you twice — your step, your face — a particular Reaper waits. He will not wait again.

mages_drowning_song.name: The Mage's Drowning Song
mages_drowning_song.flavor: Only a mage hears the harmonic the silt-drowners cast in. Three of them, sung silent. The reach will sleep one count quieter.

cleric_lay_the_threshers.name: Lay the Threshers Down
cleric_lay_the_threshers.reward_chronicle_line: They walked the Sodden Reach and laid six threshers properly down, the way the kingdom's old clerics laid the field-dead.
cleric_lay_the_threshers.flavor: The threshers were farmers once, drowned at their work. Six. Only a cleric's hand can lay them down so they stay.

reborn_a_grain_you_planted.name: A Grain You Once Planted
reborn_a_grain_you_planted.reward_chronicle_line: A previous one of them had planted a grain in Hólvar Reach, and when they came back, the field gave it back to them.
reborn_a_grain_you_planted.flavor: A previous you planted a grain here, the year before the drowning. One thresher holds the place. Take it. The grain is still there.

dark_slimes_in_the_throat.name: Dark Slimes in the Throat
dark_slimes_in_the_throat.flavor: Six of the slimes cling to the gullet wall and grow when no one is looking. Six is the number the older miners chose.

cave_trolls_at_the_chokepoint.name: Cave-Trolls at the Chokepoint
cave_trolls_at_the_chokepoint.flavor: Three trolls keep the chokepoint. Three of them, and a kingdom's worth of grain might still be brought through.

giant_bats_at_dusk.name: Giant Bats at Dusk
giant_bats_at_dusk.flavor: The carrion bats come out at dusk in eights. Take eight. The gullet will breathe one count quieter.

the_maw_mother_must_starve.name: The Maw-Mother Must Starve
the_maw_mother_must_starve.reward_chronicle_line: They walked the Gullet to make the Maw-Mother go hungry, and she did.
the_maw_mother_must_starve.flavor: The kingdom fed her once. She has not eaten since. End it.

bat_wings_for_the_choirs_powder.name: Bat-Wings for the Choir's Powder
bat_wings_for_the_choirs_powder.flavor: The Choir's old powder needs twelve. The Gullet has them. Bring.

maw_a_read_the_ledger.name: The Maw-Mother's Names — Read the Ledger
maw_a_read_the_ledger.flavor: The ledger names three bats she sent to find her food. Three of them. The Maw-Mother will know who reads.

maw_b_find_the_throat.name: The Maw-Mother's Names — Find Her Throat
maw_b_find_the_throat.flavor: Four slimes line the throat she swallows through. After them, the way to her is clear.

maw_c_unname_her.name: The Maw-Mother's Names — Unname Her
maw_c_unname_her.reward_chronicle_line: They walked the Gullet's throat and unnamed the Maw-Mother — the kingdom had three names for her, and the climber took all three.
maw_c_unname_her.flavor: The Maw-Mother. One blow at a time, the three names come off her. Then she will be only herself, and only briefly.

after_you_dropped_a_word.name: After You Dropped a Word
after_you_dropped_a_word.flavor: Once you dropped a word into the throat, the throat learned it. Now one troll wears it on his teeth. Take the troll. Take the word.

after_the_kettle.name: After You Helped Carry the Kettle
after_the_kettle.flavor: You carried a kettle for an older hand once. The slimes remember the smell. Two of them. The older hand will set a thing aside.

status_cleared_in_the_gullet.name: Walk Clean from the Slime-Pit
status_cleared_in_the_gullet.flavor: Two slimes, and when you walk out, no status clings to you. The Gullet measures who came through it clean.

no_potions_in_the_gullet.name: No Potions in the Gullet
no_potions_in_the_gullet.flavor: Two trolls. No potion crossed your lips while you were in the Gullet. The old miners did it this way to spare the supply.

hidden_gullet_first_breath.name: The Gullet Took Your First Breath
hidden_gullet_first_breath.flavor: The first time you breathed in the Gullet, one bat answered. It has been keeping the breath. Take it.

hidden_lost_verse_in_the_throat.name: The Lost Verse in the Throat
hidden_lost_verse_in_the_throat.reward_chronicle_line: Once they knew the lost verse, the Gullet's first slime sang it back to them — and they ended the singing.
hidden_lost_verse_in_the_throat.flavor: Once you know the lost verse, one slime in the Gullet hums it. Take the slime. The verse is yours alone again.

warriors_choke_three.name: The Warrior's Three at the Choke
warriors_choke_three.flavor: Only a warrior holds the chokepoint long enough. Three trolls. The kingdom's old guard did it this way for one hundred winters.

rogues_quiet_six_bats.name: The Rogue's Quiet Six
rogues_quiet_six_bats.flavor: Six bats, and not one of them sees you before you take it. Only a rogue moves through the gullet that quietly.

after_atrel_peace_in_the_gullet.name: After Atrél's Peace, the Gullet Remembers
after_atrel_peace_in_the_gullet.reward_chronicle_line: After they had once made Atrél's Peace, the Gullet's Maw-Mother knew them on sight, and braced. They took her anyway.
after_atrel_peace_in_the_gullet.flavor: Across runs, Atrél's Peace marks you. The Maw-Mother knows you from before the peace. She braces, this time. End her anyway.

pall_magistrates_three.name: Three Pall-Magistrates Will Hold No More Court
pall_magistrates_three.flavor: Three magistrates still hold court in the empty halls. End their sittings. The dead deserve a verdict.

hollow_procession_eight.name: The Procession of Eight
hollow_procession_eight.flavor: Eight hollow ones walk the funeral lap. They have walked it since the rite. End the lap.

gutter_wretches_six.name: Six Gutters of the Capital
gutter_wretches_six.flavor: Six wretches in the gutters. The capital's old guard cleared them every dawn. Clear them once.

the_hollow_bellward_must_fall.name: The Hollow Bellward Must Fall
the_hollow_bellward_must_fall.reward_chronicle_line: They climbed to the high bell of Mourncross and ended the Bellward who had been ringing it for a hundred winters.
the_hollow_bellward_must_fall.flavor: The bell has rung for a hundred winters and no one has come. End the ringer. The bell will not need to ring after.

census_a_read_the_census.name: The Census Climber — Read the Census
census_a_read_the_census.flavor: Two wretches squat on the census-stones. Take them. The names underneath are still legible.

census_b_add_your_name.name: The Census Climber — Add Your Name
census_b_add_your_name.flavor: A magistrate guards the census-quill. Take him. Add your name to the list of the kingdom that still remembers itself.

census_b_strike_it_through.name: The Census Climber — Strike It Through
census_b_strike_it_through.flavor: A magistrate guards the census-quill. Take him. Strike your name through the list. The Pall hears names; refuse to be heard.

census_c_added_keep_walking.name: The Census Climber — The Witness Keeps Walking
census_c_added_keep_walking.reward_chronicle_line: They added their name to the census of Mourncross, and three hollow ones stepped out of the procession to greet a witness.
census_c_added_keep_walking.flavor: You added your name. The procession knows a witness when it sees one. Three of them step out to test you. End the test.

census_c_struck_keep_walking.name: The Census Climber — The Refused Keeps Walking
census_c_struck_keep_walking.reward_chronicle_line: They struck their name through the census of Mourncross, and three hollow ones stepped out of the procession to greet someone who would not be greeted.
census_c_struck_keep_walking.flavor: You struck your name. The procession knows a refusal when it sees one. Three of them step out to test the refusal. End them.

bellwards_card_to_eron.name: The Bellward's Card for Eron
bellwards_card_to_eron.flavor: The Bellward kept a card for his son Eron, in his pocket. Two of the procession carry copies. Find the original.

lampkeepers_oil.name: The Lampkeeper's Oil
lampkeepers_oil.flavor: The lampkeeper's notebook names four wretches who carry her stolen oil-flasks. Take them. The lamps will be lit again.

the_scuffmark_walks_again.name: The Scuffmark Walks Again
the_scuffmark_walks_again.flavor: The scuffmarks on the cobbles match a single procession's gait. Find the gait. End the procession. The cobbles will be still.

after_the_chair_was_set_out.name: After a Widow Set a Chair Out
after_the_chair_was_set_out.flavor: Once a widow in Mourncross set a chair out for you. A magistrate wants the chair as evidence. He will not have it.

after_the_house_was_lit.name: After an Empty House Was Lit
after_the_house_was_lit.reward_chronicle_line: An empty house had lit for them once; they returned and the Hollow Bellward rang her bell, and they ended her ringing.
after_the_house_was_lit.flavor: Once you saw an empty house in Mourncross with a lit window. The Bellward saw it too. End her, and the light stays.

companion_kill_a_magistrate.name: Let the Sworn Strike the Last Blow
companion_kill_a_magistrate.flavor: Bring your sworn. Bring the magistrate down to nothing. The last blow belongs to your sworn. The Pall hates a sworn-kill.

first_strike_in_the_capital.name: First Strike, Mourncross
first_strike_in_the_capital.flavor: Three of the procession, and the first blow in each fight is yours. The Pall hears who struck first.

after_old_seal_in_the_capital.name: After the Old Seal, the Capital Knows You
after_old_seal_in_the_capital.reward_chronicle_line: Once they had once made The Old Seal, the Hollow Bellward met their second phase as a father, and they ended him anyway.
after_old_seal_in_the_capital.flavor: Across runs, The Old Seal marks you. The Hollow Bellward's second phase is a father, now, not a Bellward. End him anyway.

clerics_funeral_for_a_capital.name: The Funeral the Capital Never Had
clerics_funeral_for_a_capital.reward_chronicle_line: A cleric walked Mourncross and gave seven of the procession a funeral. The kingdom's old clerics would have done the same.
clerics_funeral_for_a_capital.flavor: Seven of the procession. A cleric's hand is the only hand that lays them down so they stay laid.

choirmaster_effigies_three.name: Three Choirmaster Effigies
choirmaster_effigies_three.flavor: Three of the effigies still raise the choir each morning. End the raising. The morning will be quiet.

reliquary_golems_two.name: The Reliquary Golems Will Yield
reliquary_golems_two.flavor: Two of the golems hold the choir's relics. Take the holders. The relics belong to no one and that is right.

censer_shades_five.name: Five Censer-Shades for the Smoke
censer_shades_five.flavor: Five censer-shades fill the choir with smoke that will not settle. End them. The air will be air.

cantor_vael_must_close_his_mouth.name: Cantor Vael Must Close His Mouth
cantor_vael_must_close_his_mouth.reward_chronicle_line: They walked the Unanswered Choir and closed the mouth of Cantor Vael, who had been singing the rite of unremembering without pause since the kingdom forgot itself.
cantor_vael_must_close_his_mouth.flavor: He has been singing the rite since the kingdom forgot itself. He has not eaten. He has not slept. End the singing.

mouth_a_read_the_rite.name: The Mouth That Will Not Close — Read the Rite
mouth_a_read_the_rite.flavor: The rite is on the wall, half-burned. Two censer-shades guard the legible half. Read what you can. Take the shades.

mouth_b_learn_the_lost_verse.name: The Mouth That Will Not Close — The Lost Verse
mouth_b_learn_the_lost_verse.flavor: The lost verse is the third line of the rite. An effigy still sings it. Take the effigy. The third line will be yours.

mouth_c_pall_speaks.name: The Mouth That Will Not Close — The Pall Speaks
mouth_c_pall_speaks.flavor: A golem keeps the reliquary that holds the Pall's only words. Take the golem. Read what the Pall has said only once.

mouth_d_close_the_mouth.name: The Mouth That Will Not Close — Close It
mouth_d_close_the_mouth.reward_chronicle_line: They knew the rite, the lost verse, and the Pall's only words; they closed Cantor Vael's mouth and the kingdom heard the third line for the first time in a hundred winters.
mouth_d_close_the_mouth.flavor: You know the rite. You know the lost verse. You know what the Pall has said. Close the Cantor's mouth. Sing the third line back at him. The kingdom will hear it once more.

palls_only_words_taken.name: The Words the Pall Has Spoken
palls_only_words_taken.flavor: A golem keeps the reliquary that holds the Pall's only spoken words. Take it. The words are not for the Pall to keep.

cantors_last_sermon_heard.name: The Cantor's Last Sermon
cantors_last_sermon_heard.flavor: Three censer-shades carry the smoke of the Cantor's last sermon. Take them. The smoke can dissipate.

atrels_reply_taken_quietly.name: Atrél's Reply, Taken Quietly
atrels_reply_taken_quietly.flavor: An effigy still mocks Atrél's reply to the Pall. Take it. Atrél will have her quiet, briefly.

choir_no_damage_taken_cantor_phase_one.name: Untouched in the Choir's First Verse
choir_no_damage_taken_cantor_phase_one.flavor: Two effigies, and no harm comes to you in either fight. The Pall hates an untouched climber.

choir_critical_killing_blow.name: End Three with the Single Critical Blow
choir_critical_killing_blow.flavor: Three censer-shades, and the killing blow on each is a crit. The kingdom's old grandmasters did it this way.

choir_kept_full_stamina.name: Walk the Choir with Breath Kept
choir_kept_full_stamina.flavor: A reliquary golem. End it without spending a single point of your breath. The kingdom's old monks did it this way.

choir_both_warmth_and_recognition.name: The Choir Warmed and the Cantor Recognized You
choir_both_warmth_and_recognition.reward_chronicle_line: Once the Choir had warmed for them and the Cantor had recognized their step, they took the Cantor anyway — and he died warm.
choir_both_warmth_and_recognition.flavor: The Choir has warmed for you. The Cantor has recognized your step. Both marks. End him anyway. He dies warm.

after_purify_in_the_choir.name: After the Purify, the Choir Sings Lower
after_purify_in_the_choir.flavor: Once a previous you had purified Mournhold. The Cantor sings lower for you, in apology. End him anyway.

after_atrel_peace_at_the_side_altar.name: After Atrél's Peace, Set a Grief at the Side Altar
after_atrel_peace_at_the_side_altar.flavor: Once you have made Atrél's Peace, the side altar accepts a grief. An effigy guards the altar. End it. Set the grief.

mountain_ogres_four.name: Four Ogres on the Bare Ridge
mountain_ogres_four.flavor: Four ogres watch the bare ridge — the spot where climbers stop seeing each other's silhouettes against the sky. End them.

stone_golems_two.name: Stone-Golems at the Step
stone_golems_two.flavor: Two stone-golems hold the last narrow step. Without them, the way to the Summit becomes a thing a climber can walk.

ice_wraiths_six.name: Six Wraiths Above the Snowline
ice_wraiths_six.flavor: Six wraiths above the snowline. They were climbers once and the snow is colder for not knowing it.

the_ashen_penitent_must_kneel.name: The Ashen Penitent Must Kneel
the_ashen_penitent_must_kneel.reward_chronicle_line: They walked the Ashen Climb to the Penitent's stone and made him kneel one last time.
the_ashen_penitent_must_kneel.flavor: He was the first survivor who turned back. He has knelt at this stone for a century. Make him kneel one more time.

hand_a_find_the_handprint.name: The Hand Beneath Yours — Find the Print
hand_a_find_the_handprint.flavor: The climbers' stone has a handprint that fits yours. Two wraiths circle it. End them. Place your hand.

hand_b_climb_the_step.name: The Hand Beneath Yours — Climb the Step
hand_b_climb_the_step.flavor: A stone-golem stands at the step the hand under yours could not pass. Pass it. The hand under yours is owed.

hand_c_set_your_print.name: The Hand Beneath Yours — Set Your Own
hand_c_set_your_print.reward_chronicle_line: They placed their own handprint above the one from before, and the Penitent recognized the order of the hands.
hand_c_set_your_print.flavor: The Penitent waits at the upper stone. Take him. Then set your own handprint above the one from before. The order will matter, later.

piranesi_bare_patch_known.name: The Bare Patch Piranesi Drew
piranesi_bare_patch_known.flavor: Piranesi drew the bare patch as the place where the snow refuses to fall. An ogre keeps that patch. Take it.

penitents_paper_read.name: What the Penitent's Paper Names
penitents_paper_read.flavor: The paper names his wife. Once you have read the name, the Penitent will allow himself one true fight. End him in it.

after_he_named_you.name: After He Named You
after_he_named_you.reward_chronicle_line: After the Penitent had named them, they returned to his stone and ended him with the name he had given them.
after_he_named_you.flavor: He named you once. Return to the stone. End him with the name he gave you. Names should not outlive their givers.

after_his_wifes_name.name: After You Knew His Wife's Name
after_his_wifes_name.flavor: Once you knew his wife's name. The Penitent owes you one true name in return — the Summit's. End him. Carry the name up.

climb_low_hp_kill.name: The Last Hour of the Climb
climb_low_hp_kill.flavor: Take a stone-golem while your hp is below a quarter. The old climbers said it was the only honest way to end one.

climb_no_healing.name: The Climber Who Refused Healing
climb_no_healing.flavor: Two wraiths. No healing received in either fight — not a potion, not a companion. The kingdom's old climbers walked this way until they could not.

climb_no_abilities.name: Without the Things They Taught You
climb_no_abilities.flavor: An ogre. No ability used the whole fight. Sword and feet, the way the kingdom's first climbers did. Honest.

hidden_penitent_was_waiting.name: The Penitent Was Waiting
hidden_penitent_was_waiting.flavor: He has been kneeling at this stone for a century, and the shape he kneels around is your shape. End him.

after_reckoning_on_the_climb.name: After the Reckoning, the Climb Remembers
after_reckoning_on_the_climb.reward_chronicle_line: Once they had once unmade Mournhold by Reckoning, the Ashen Penitent at the stone above the snowline turned his head and asked for the rest. They gave it.
after_reckoning_on_the_climb.flavor: Across runs, the Reckoning marks you. The Penitent turns his head when you arrive. End him. The kingdom never had a way to refuse this.

bonesong_quartet.name: Four Bonesongs to Quiet
bonesong_quartet.flavor: Four bonesongs hum at the corners of the holds. End the humming. The dead in the walls deserve a different sound.

doorshut_three.name: Three Doorshuts Will Open Once
doorshut_three.flavor: Three doorshuts are the doors that closed the holds against the kingdom. End them. The doors will open once.

red_beards_three.name: Three Red Beards on the Old Hammer-Road
red_beards_three.flavor: Three of the holds' old guard still walk the hammer-road. End them. The hammer-road belongs to no one and that is right.

reedwives_two.name: Two Reedwives at the Old Wash
reedwives_two.flavor: Two reedwives at the old wash, still trying to clean what should not be clean. End them. The wash can be wash again.

doorposts_a_read_the_sentry_log.name: The Names on the Doorposts — Read the Sentry Log
doorposts_a_read_the_sentry_log.flavor: The sentry log names two doorshuts who closed first. End them. Their names are the first you will need.

doorposts_b_seed_ledger.name: The Names on the Doorposts — The Seed Ledger
doorposts_b_seed_ledger.flavor: The seed ledger names a Red Beard who refused to share. End him. The ledger will name him a refuser.

doorposts_c_brackmere_child.name: The Names on the Doorposts — Brackmere's Child
doorposts_c_brackmere_child.flavor: The Brackmere child's name is on a reedwife's apron. End her. Take the apron. The child will be named again.

doorposts_d_carve_a_name.name: The Names on the Doorposts — Carve a Name
doorposts_d_carve_a_name.reward_chronicle_line: They walked the Drowned Holds and named four doorposts — sentry, refuser, child, and grandmother — and the Holds had four names they could be remembered by, briefly.
doorposts_d_carve_a_name.flavor: Three bonesongs guard the doorpost where you can carve the names. End them. Carve. The kingdom will have four names the Pall did not unname.

red_beards_hammer_picked_up.name: Red Beard's Hammer Haft, Reclaimed
red_beards_hammer_picked_up.flavor: His haft is still in the doorpost. End the Red Beard who left it there. The haft will not need to stay.

after_holds_blamed_you.name: After the Holds Blamed You for Living
after_holds_blamed_you.flavor: The Holds blamed you for living. Three bonesongs sing the blame. End them. The blame will be a grief instead.

after_dead_hand_caught_sleeve.name: After a Dead Hand Caught Your Sleeve
after_dead_hand_caught_sleeve.reward_chronicle_line: Once a dead hand had caught their sleeve in the Drowned Holds, they returned and ended the reedwife who had thrown it.
after_dead_hand_caught_sleeve.flavor: Once a dead hand caught your sleeve here. A reedwife threw it. End her. The sleeve will be a sleeve.

named_them_at_death_holds.name: Name Them As They Die
named_them_at_death_holds.flavor: Two doorshuts. Speak their old name as the killing blow lands. The kingdom's old undertakers did it this way.

no_damage_taken_holds.name: Clean Through the Drowned Holds
no_damage_taken_holds.flavor: A Red Beard. End him without a scratch on you. The Holds measure who came through them whole.

hidden_forager_recognized_you.name: The Forager Boy Recognized You
hidden_forager_recognized_you.flavor: The forager boy in the rafters looked at you for one count longer than at the others. End the boy. The look will mean something later.

reborn_you_were_almost_a_forager.name: A Previous You Was Almost a Forager Boy
reborn_you_were_almost_a_forager.reward_chronicle_line: A previous one of them had been a forager boy in the Drowned Holds, and when they returned, one of the boys had the same shard of pottery in his pocket.
reborn_you_were_almost_a_forager.flavor: A previous you stood in this hold once, hand on a windowsill, near to becoming a forager. One of the boys has the shard your previous you almost picked up. End him. Take the shard.

mages_four_names.name: The Mage's Four Counter-Songs
mages_four_names.reward_chronicle_line: A mage walked the Drowned Holds and pitched a counter-song against four bonesongs. The names they had eaten came back out of their mouths in the dying.
mages_four_names.flavor: Four bonesongs each carry a name they ate. Only a mage knows the right pitch to make them give the names back.

pack_lead_must_fall.name: The Pack Lead Will Yield
pack_lead_must_fall.flavor: The pack lead has held the cache since the kingdom's last hunters left. End the holding. The cache will open.

hunters_cache_three_step_a.name: The Hunter's Cache — Read the Lore
hunters_cache_three_step_a.flavor: Three wolves at the wood-edge before the cache opens. End them. The lore inside will be readable next.

hunters_cache_three_step_b.name: The Hunter's Cache — Match the Collars
hunters_cache_three_step_b.flavor: Two wolves wear collars matching the cache's. End them. The pack will understand who the new lead is.

hunters_cache_three_step_c.name: The Hunter's Cache — Take the Lead
hunters_cache_three_step_c.reward_chronicle_line: They read the cache's lore, matched its collars, and took the pack lead. The kingdom's last hunters would have nodded.
hunters_cache_three_step_c.flavor: The pack lead has been waiting. End him. The cache is yours.

dyke_warden_three.name: Three Dyke-Wardens Will Open the Sluice
dyke_warden_three.flavor: Three of the wardens hold the sluice closed. End them. The Reach beyond will drain by one count.

dyke_after_third_beams_read.name: After Reading the Third Dyke's Beams
dyke_after_third_beams_read.reward_chronicle_line: Once they had read the third dyke's beam-marks, they ended the warden who had carved them and read his name off his sleeve.
dyke_after_third_beams_read.flavor: The beams of the third dyke name the warden who carved them. End him. The carving will mean only what it meant.

library_warden_six.name: Six Library-Wardens for the Catalog
library_warden_six.flavor: Six wardens have kept the burned catalog out of climbers' hands for a century. End them. The catalog will be readable.

library_after_clerks_seal.name: After You Saw the Clerk's Seal Register
library_after_clerks_seal.flavor: Two wardens guard the page the clerk's seal marks. End them. The page will be a page.

pallid_stag_thrice.name: The Pallid Stag, Met Three Times
pallid_stag_thrice.flavor: The Stag returns when the Witherwood is sufficiently cleansed. Three times, three meetings. The Stag will not return after.

the_forgotten_thing_once.name: End the Forgotten Thing
the_forgotten_thing_once.reward_chronicle_line: They found the thing the Pall had forgotten and ended it — the Pall did not notice that one of its own had been lost.
the_forgotten_thing_once.flavor: The Pall forgot one of its own. The thing waits in the Witherwood, not knowing it should not exist. End it. The Pall will not know.

chairman_must_close_his_books.name: The Chairman Must Close His Books
chairman_must_close_his_books.reward_chronicle_line: They walked the Sealed Chamber and ended the Chairman, who had been closing the kingdom's books in order to close the kingdom.
chairman_must_close_his_books.flavor: He sealed the chamber with himself inside, and continued the rite from there. End him. The chamber will be unsealed.

library_no_abilities.name: The Honest Librarian's Way
library_no_abilities.flavor: A library-warden. No ability used the whole fight. The clerks who served before the burning did it this way.

dyke_first_strike.name: First Strike at the Last Dyke
dyke_first_strike.flavor: Two dyke-wardens. The first blow in each fight is yours. The kingdom's old water-keepers struck first or lost.

pallid_after_first_grey.name: The Pallid Stag, After the First Grey
pallid_after_first_grey.reward_chronicle_line: After their first grey hair, the Pallid Stag let them have the kill. Some kills are gifts and some are losses; this was both.
pallid_after_first_grey.flavor: Once you have the first grey hair, the Pallid Stag knows. He will not refuse you. End him. The wood will know.

hidden_library_remembers_a_reader.name: The Library Remembers a Reader
hidden_library_remembers_a_reader.flavor: Once you have read the Burned Library's lore, a warden remembers the last person who read it. End the warden. The reader and the warden will both be done.

clerics_warden_funeral.name: A Funeral for the Wardens
clerics_warden_funeral.reward_chronicle_line: A cleric walked the Burned Library and gave three of its wardens a funeral. The clerks who served before would have nodded.
clerics_warden_funeral.flavor: Three wardens. Only a cleric can give them the funeral the clerks were owed. End them. Lay them down properly.

wynne_devoured_captain.name: The Devoured Captain Will Eat No More
wynne_devoured_captain.reward_chronicle_line: They walked Wynne's silent camp and ended the Devoured Captain who had been eating his own to feed the Pall.
wynne_devoured_captain.flavor: He was Wynne's captain. The Pall took his appetite first. End him. The camp will be silent the way camps are meant to be.

wynne_cooking_fire_callback.name: After Wynne's Cooking Fire Stayed Warm
wynne_cooking_fire_callback.flavor: A cooking fire at Wynne's stayed warm for you, once. The Captain remembers. End him. The fire will be a fire.

wynne_after_emissary_letter.name: After the Emissary Left a Letter at Wynne's
wynne_after_emissary_letter.reward_chronicle_line: An emissary had left a letter at Wynne's silent camp once; the climber returned, ended the Devoured Captain, and the letter was finally allowed to be read.
wynne_after_emissary_letter.flavor: The emissary's letter was waiting for you, behind the Captain. End the Captain. The letter has been waiting too long.

margrave_censor_ends_the_censoring.name: The Margrave Censor Must Read No Names
margrave_censor_ends_the_censoring.reward_chronicle_line: They walked the Margrave's Monument and ended the Censor who had been crossing out names from the kingdom's own census for a hundred winters.
margrave_censor_ends_the_censoring.flavor: He has been crossing names off the kingdom's own list. End him. The names he had not yet crossed will keep their letters.

margrave_after_real_census.name: After You Read the Real Census
margrave_after_real_census.flavor: The real census names the climber the Margrave is afraid of. End the Margrave. The fear was justified, briefly.

margrave_after_seal.name: After His Seal Stayed Warm Under Your Palm
margrave_after_seal.flavor: His seal stayed warm under your palm. The Margrave knows the warmth. End him. The seal will know to whom it now belongs.

shrine_keeper_three.name: End the Shrine Keeper's Watch
shrine_keeper_three.reward_chronicle_line: They walked the Pre-Pall Shrine and ended the Keeper three times — for the first king, for the first verse, and for the first climber's handprint.
shrine_keeper_three.flavor: He has been keeping the shrine since before the Pall — since before the kingdom had a name to lose. End his keeping. Three times. He returns each time.

shrine_after_verren_fragment.name: After You Held Verren's Fragment
shrine_after_verren_fragment.flavor: Verren's fragment names the Keeper as one who served before her. End him. Verren's fragment is owed a count of silence.

shrine_after_first_kings_pommel.name: After You Took the First King's Pommel
shrine_after_first_kings_pommel.flavor: The first king's pommel is small in your palm. The Keeper recognizes the weight. End him. The pommel will not be lost.

shrine_after_handprint.name: After You Saw the First Climber's Handprint
shrine_after_handprint.reward_chronicle_line: They saw the first climber's handprint at the Pre-Pall Shrine and ended the Keeper. The handprint had been waiting for a second handprint to be set above it.
shrine_after_handprint.flavor: The first climber's handprint is in the stone. End the Keeper. Set your hand. The print under yours will know.

summit_after_warden_ending.name: Return to the Summit, After the Warden
summit_after_warden_ending.reward_chronicle_line: Once they had once become the Warden, a previous one of them returned to the Summit and ended their own previous self — and the Pall blinked, briefly.
summit_after_warden_ending.flavor: Once you have once become the Warden, a previous you keeps the Summit. End them. The Pall blinks. The blink is enough.

summit_after_purify_ending.name: Return to the Summit, After the Purify
summit_after_purify_ending.flavor: Once you have purified Mournhold, the Warden at the Summit is the kingdom's own self standing in for the Pall. End them. The kingdom will know its own face.

hidden_shrine_first_visit.name: The Shrine Has Been Waiting
hidden_shrine_first_visit.flavor: The Pre-Pall Shrine has been waiting since before the Pall. The Keeper turns his head when you cross the threshold. End him. The Shrine will be older than you again.

margrave_unarmed_kill.name: The Margrave, Unarmed
margrave_unarmed_kill.flavor: End the Margrave with no weapon equipped. The kingdom's old censors were ended this way when they had crossed too many.

wynne_first_strike.name: First Strike at Wynne's
wynne_first_strike.flavor: End the Devoured Captain after striking first. The Pall hates anyone who strikes before being struck.

reborn_summit_keeper_remembers.name: A Previous You Climbed Here Once
reborn_summit_keeper_remembers.reward_chronicle_line: A previous one of them had finished the climb. The Summit recognized their step. They ended the Warden the way previous selves had once been ended.
reborn_summit_keeper_remembers.flavor: A previous you climbed here. The Summit knows your step. End the Warden. The climb will know the name the previous you carried.

after_mira_recipe.name: After Mira's Recipe
after_mira_recipe.flavor: Mira's recipe asks for three wolves' worth of clean fat. Bring them. She will bake. The Hold will eat.

after_lis_made_it.name: After Lis Made It
after_lis_made_it.flavor: Lis made a small thing. Four goblins took the small things. End them. Lis will know.

after_small_uns_drawing.name: After the Small One's Drawing
after_small_uns_drawing.flavor: The small one drew three bandits, each with a different face. End them. The drawing will be a finished drawing.

after_talvas_case.name: After Tálva's Case
after_talvas_case.reward_chronicle_line: Once they had read Tálva's case, they walked back to Mourncross and ended the magistrate who had heard it last.
after_talvas_case.flavor: Tálva's case names the magistrate who heard it last. End him. The case will be a closed case.

after_ondreks_spoon.name: After You Held Ondrek's Spoon
after_ondreks_spoon.flavor: Ondrek's spoon is in your pack. Two threshers in the Reach ate from spoons like this. End them. Ondrek will know.

hold_walk_a_wolf.name: The Hold's Walk Begins — A Wolf
hold_walk_a_wolf.flavor: Mira's cloth has a wolf-hair on it. Find the wolf. The Hold is walking back through the kingdom; you walk with it.

hold_walk_b_thresher.name: The Hold's Walk — A Thresher
hold_walk_b_thresher.flavor: The cloth has silt on its hem. A thresher in the Reach. End it. The cloth knows where it has been.

hold_walk_c_slime.name: The Hold's Walk — A Slime
hold_walk_c_slime.flavor: There is salt in the cloth's weave — the Gullet's salt. A slime ate part of it. End it. The cloth will be cleaner.

hold_walk_d_procession.name: The Hold's Walk — A Procession
hold_walk_d_procession.reward_chronicle_line: Mira's cloth had walked the kingdom — wolf, thresher, slime, procession. They walked the same path, and the cloth was clean.
hold_walk_d_procession.flavor: The cloth's last thread points to a Mourncross procession. End one. The cloth was once a kingdom; it has walked through one again.

after_lost_verse_4.name: After You Knew the Fourth Lost Verse
after_lost_verse_4.flavor: Two censer-shades carry the fourth lost verse in their smoke. End them. The verse will be in air instead.

after_bone_tomb_seals.name: After the Column of Seals
after_bone_tomb_seals.flavor: The Bone Tomb's column of seals names a Choir golem. End it. The column will lose one rune; the kingdom will gain one breath.

after_border_lichen.name: After the Border Lichen Post
after_border_lichen.flavor: The Border lichen grows on the post the same way it grows on a Penitent's stone. End the Penitent. The Border will know it is bordered.

after_karst_grain.name: After You Took a Karst Grain
after_karst_grain.flavor: Karst's grain is in your pocket. Two threshers in the Reach would have eaten it. End them. The grain will be planted.

after_tace_sled_rail.name: After You Held Tace's Sled-Rail
after_tace_sled_rail.flavor: Tace's sled-rail is iron-cold in your hand. Three ice-wraiths above the snowline used to be sled-mates of Tace's. End them.

after_readers_pencil.name: After the Reader's Pencil
after_readers_pencil.flavor: The reader's pencil has been sharpened for one warden. End him. The reader will sharpen again, for a kinder use.

after_small_altar.name: After the Small Altar at Gravewatch
after_small_altar.reward_chronicle_line: Once they had touched the small altar at Gravewatch, the Pre-Pall Shrine's Keeper turned his head when they entered, and they ended him without ceremony.
after_small_altar.flavor: The small altar at Gravewatch's edge marks the climber. The Pre-Pall Shrine's Keeper turns his head. End him.

after_hearthkeepers_book.name: After the Hearthkeeper's Book
after_hearthkeepers_book.flavor: The Hearthkeeper's book names four wolves who killed her first hearth. End them. The hearth will warm without them.

hidden_after_insomniac_tally.name: The Insomniac's Tally Has Your Name
hidden_after_insomniac_tally.flavor: The insomniac's tally counts you among the climbers who did not sleep this winter. Two bandits believe it. End them.

after_witherwood_tree.name: After Piranesi's Witherwood Tree
after_witherwood_tree.flavor: Piranesi's drawing of the tree marks two wolves that pace around it. End them. The tree will not be paced again.

after_atrel_marker_wood.name: After Atrél's Marker in the Wood
after_atrel_marker_wood.flavor: Atrél's marker names three bandits who passed it without kneeling. End them. The marker will get a kneeling later.

stag_a_first_meeting.name: The Pallid Stag — First Meeting
stag_a_first_meeting.flavor: Two wolves pace at the clearing where the Stag will appear. End them. The clearing will be ready for a first meeting.

stag_b_second_meeting.name: The Pallid Stag — Second Meeting
stag_b_second_meeting.flavor: Three goblins keep the clearing from settling. End them. The Stag will return for a second meeting.

stag_c_third_meeting.name: The Pallid Stag — Third Meeting
stag_c_third_meeting.reward_chronicle_line: They met the Pallid Stag three times in the Witherwood. The third meeting was a teaching, not a fight, and yet they took it.
stag_c_third_meeting.flavor: The Stag has come back for the third time. The third meeting is a teaching. Take the teaching. Take the Stag.

after_wolf_knows_scent.name: After the Wolf Knew Your Scent
after_wolf_knows_scent.flavor: Once a wolf knew your scent. Three of the pack now share it. End them. The scent will be yours alone again.

after_held_its_breath.name: After the Wood Held Its Breath
after_held_its_breath.reward_chronicle_line: Once the Witherwood had held its breath for them; they returned and ended two bandits the wood would have wished to keep quiet.
after_held_its_breath.flavor: The wood held its breath for you. Two bandits broke the holding. End them. The wood will breathe again.

after_forgot_pine.name: After You Forgot the Smell of Pine
after_forgot_pine.flavor: You forgot the smell of pine for a count. Three carrion bats remember the count. End them. The pine will smell again.

after_remembered_younger_step.name: After the Wood Remembered a Younger Step
after_remembered_younger_step.reward_chronicle_line: Once the Witherwood had remembered their younger step; they returned and ended the wolf that had taken note.
after_remembered_younger_step.flavor: The wood remembered your younger step. One wolf took notes. End it. The notes will be yours.

wood_no_healing_received.name: The Climber Who Refused Healing in the Wood
wood_no_healing_received.flavor: Two wolves. No healing received in either fight. The wood's old climbers walked this way until they could not.

wood_killed_after_dodging.name: End Them After the Dodge
wood_killed_after_dodging.flavor: Two bandits. Each killing blow lands on a turn after you dodged. The old rangers did it this way.

wood_pet_assisted.name: Let the Pet Bring the Goblin Down
wood_pet_assisted.flavor: Three goblins. Your pet lands the blow on each that brings them under half hp. The pet learns its name from doing.

wood_killed_with_thrown.name: Thrown, Not Swung
wood_killed_with_thrown.flavor: A wolf. Killed with a thrown weapon. The wood's old climbers did it this way for the wolves they had no quarrel with.

hidden_atrel_marker_seen.name: After Atrél's Marker, the Wood Sends Three
hidden_atrel_marker_seen.flavor: Once Atrél's marker is known to you, three goblins of the wood appear who had seen Atrél once and walked away unchanged. End them. The wood will be one count quieter.

rangers_quiet_wolves.name: The Ranger's Quiet Wolves
rangers_quiet_wolves.flavor: Five wolves. Each one taken without the rest of the pack hearing. Only a ranger walks the wood quiet enough.

rogues_silent_six.name: The Rogue's Silent Six
rogues_silent_six.flavor: Six bandits. None see you coming. Only a rogue can take six of them without the wood's birds picking up the change.

reborn_younger_step.name: A Previous You Walked Here Younger
reborn_younger_step.reward_chronicle_line: A previous one of them had walked the Witherwood younger. The wood remembered the gait. The new self ended the wolf the old self had run from.
reborn_younger_step.flavor: A previous you walked here younger and faster. One wolf the previous you ran from is still there. End it.

thresher_pair_at_low_water.name: Two Threshers at Low Water
thresher_pair_at_low_water.flavor: Two threshers at low water — the easy two, the ones the Reach offers you when you arrive. End them. The Reach begins the day quieter.

silt_drowners_at_dawn.name: Silt-Drowners at Dawn
silt_drowners_at_dawn.flavor: Four silt-drowners come up at dawn. The Reach's old fishermen took them while the songs were still warm. End them.

thresher_reeds_for_winter.name: Thresher-Reeds for the Long Winter
thresher_reeds_for_winter.reward_chronicle_line: They brought back twenty thresher-reeds — enough to seal the Reach's last farmhouse through one more winter.
thresher_reeds_for_winter.flavor: The last farmhouse needs twenty reeds to seal it for the winter. Twenty. No fewer. The roof leaked once already.

after_silt_in_boots.name: After the Silt in Your Boots
after_silt_in_boots.flavor: Silt sat in your boots after the Reach. Two threshers walked past it without noticing. End them. The silt will teach you to wade after.

after_river_hairband.name: After the River Took Your Hairband
after_river_hairband.flavor: The river took your hairband once. A silt-drowner wears it on his wrist. End him. The hairband will come back.

after_kept_your_step.name: After the Reach Kept Your Step
after_kept_your_step.reward_chronicle_line: The Reach had kept their step once; they returned and ended the Bog-Lurcher whose footprints had been the kept ones, found out.
after_kept_your_step.flavor: The Reach kept your step. A Lurcher walks in the kept step. End the Lurcher. The step will be yours alone.

after_drowned_song.name: After the Reach Drowned a Song You Loved
after_drowned_song.flavor: A song you loved drowned in the Reach. The Reaper hums it now. End him. The song will be quiet.

after_helka_bench.name: After Helka Set a Bench for You
after_helka_bench.flavor: Helka set a bench for you at the Reach's edge once. Three threshers walked between you and the bench. End them. The bench will be a bench again.

reaper_no_damage.name: Clean Against the Last Reaper
reaper_no_damage.flavor: The Last Reaper. End him without a scratch on you. The Reach's old water-keepers did it this way to keep the dead uninterested.

lurcher_named_at_death.name: Name the Bog-Lurchers as They Die
lurcher_named_at_death.flavor: Two Lurchers. Speak each one's old name as the killing blow lands. The Reach's old morticians did it this way.

silt_pet_assisted.name: Let the Pet Bring the Silt-Drowners Down
silt_pet_assisted.flavor: Two silt-drowners. Your pet lands the blow that drops each under half hp. The pet learns the Reach the way pets do.

thresher_no_abilities.name: Three Threshers Without Abilities
thresher_no_abilities.flavor: Three threshers. No ability used in any of the three fights. The Reach's first farmers did it this way.

warriors_wall_against_reach.name: The Warrior's Wall Against the Reach
warriors_wall_against_reach.flavor: Six threshers, broken against the wall a warrior holds at the Reach's edge. The Reach respects the wall.

rangers_dry_foot.name: The Ranger's Dry Foot on the Reach
rangers_dry_foot.flavor: Three lurchers. The ranger never wets her foot in any of the three fights. The Reach respects dry feet.

hidden_after_piranesi_furrow.name: After Piranesi Drew the Furrow
hidden_after_piranesi_furrow.flavor: Once Piranesi's furrow drawing is known to you, two threshers walk along the furrow's exact line. End them. The furrow will be a furrow.

after_reckoning_in_the_reach.name: After the Reckoning, the Reach Is Colder
after_reckoning_in_the_reach.reward_chronicle_line: Once they had once unmade Mournhold by Reckoning, the Reach's Last Reaper was colder by one count, and they took him in that count.
after_reckoning_in_the_reach.flavor: Across runs, the Reckoning marks you. The Reach is colder by one count for you. The Reaper feels the cold. End him.

bat_wings_for_the_grave_lamp.name: Bat-Wings for the Grave Lamp
bat_wings_for_the_grave_lamp.flavor: The grave-lamp at Gravewatch needs four bat-wings to burn through the long count. Four. The lamp will burn quieter.

after_gullet_lost_a_word.name: After You Lost a Word to the Gullet's Air
after_gullet_lost_a_word.flavor: A word of yours fell into the Gullet's air. Two slimes absorbed it. End them. The word will come back to you.

after_older_hand_set_aside.name: After the Older Hand Set a Thing Aside for You
after_older_hand_set_aside.flavor: An older hand set a thing aside for you in the Gullet. A troll wants the thing for himself. End him. The thing is yours.

after_older_hand_looked_at_you.name: After the Older Hand Looked at You
after_older_hand_looked_at_you.flavor: The older hand looked at you once, and three bats took note. End them. The look will be forgotten by everyone except you.

after_found_a_button_pair.name: After You Found a Button Pair in the Gullet
after_found_a_button_pair.reward_chronicle_line: Once they had found a pair of buttons in the Gullet, they ended the slime that had nearly absorbed them and kept the pair.
after_found_a_button_pair.flavor: A pair of buttons in your pocket from the Gullet. A slime wants them whole. End the slime. The buttons stay paired.

after_gullet_marked_your_skin.name: After the Gullet Marked Your Skin
after_gullet_marked_your_skin.reward_chronicle_line: Once the Gullet had marked their skin, the Maw-Mother braced when they entered, and they took her in the bracing.
after_gullet_marked_your_skin.flavor: The Gullet's mark is on your skin. The Maw-Mother sees the mark, and braces. End her in the brace. The mark fades after.

after_kettle_carry_second.name: After You Carried a Second Kettle
after_kettle_carry_second.flavor: You carried one kettle once. The Gullet asks for a second, this time past two trolls. End them. The kettle will be the second kettle a climber has carried this winter.

gullet_companion_kill_troll.name: The Sworn Closes the Troll
gullet_companion_kill_troll.flavor: A cave-troll. Your sworn lands the killing blow. The Gullet respects sworn-kills.

gullet_fled_then_returned.name: Flee, Then Return
gullet_fled_then_returned.flavor: A slime. Flee the first fight. Return. End it. The Gullet respects climbers who have learned the trick of leaving.

gullet_killed_during_stun.name: End Them While They Are Stunned
gullet_killed_during_stun.flavor: Two bats. The killing blow lands while each is stunned. The kingdom's old hunters did it this way for cleaner pelts.

gullet_no_potions_in_zone.name: No Potions in the Gullet, Trolls Edition
gullet_no_potions_in_zone.flavor: A troll. No potion crossed your lips while in the Gullet — harder this time, because the troll bites deep.

rogues_silent_slimes.name: The Rogue's Silent Slimes
rogues_silent_slimes.flavor: Five slimes, none of them aware. Only a rogue moves through the Gullet that quietly.

rangers_trail_finder.name: The Ranger's Trail Through the Gullet
rangers_trail_finder.flavor: Two trolls. The ranger finds the trail that lets her take both without doubling back. The Gullet's old guides did it this way.

hidden_older_hand_witnessed.name: The Older Hand Witnessed Your Step
hidden_older_hand_witnessed.flavor: The older hand in the Gullet sees you step. Two bats notice the older hand noticing. End them. The hand will see again.

after_atrel_peace_maw_quieter.name: After Atrél's Peace, the Maw Is Quieter
after_atrel_peace_maw_quieter.reward_chronicle_line: Once they had made Atrél's Peace, the Maw-Mother was quieter by one count when she fell, and they could hear themselves think.
after_atrel_peace_maw_quieter.flavor: Across runs, Atrél's Peace marks you. The Maw-Mother is quieter by one count for you. End her. The quiet remains.

after_rope_in_palm.name: After the Bellward's Rope Was in Your Palm
after_rope_in_palm.reward_chronicle_line: Once the Bellward's rope had been in their palm, they returned and ended the Bellward who had handed it to them.
after_rope_in_palm.flavor: The Bellward's rope passed through your palm once. He knows. Return. End him. The rope stays unrung after.

after_capital_air.name: After You Breathed the Capital Air
after_capital_air.flavor: You breathed Mourncross's air. Two magistrates notice the breath. End them. The air will be one count cleaner.

after_one_hour_of_light.name: After Your One Hour of Light
after_one_hour_of_light.flavor: Mourncross gave you one hour of light. Three of the procession walked through it. End them. The hour will return.

after_door_opened_count.name: After a Door Opened for a Count
after_door_opened_count.flavor: A door opened for one count for you in Mourncross. Two wretches stepped through after. End them. The door will close properly.

after_unnamed_grave_called.name: After an Unnamed Grave Called Your Name
after_unnamed_grave_called.reward_chronicle_line: Once an unnamed grave in Mourncross had called their name, they returned and ended the Bellward who had once made the grave unnamable.
after_unnamed_grave_called.flavor: An unnamed grave called your name once. The Bellward heard the calling. End her. The grave will know one name now.

after_chairman_looked.name: After the Chairman Looked at You
after_chairman_looked.flavor: The Chairman looked at you. A magistrate witnessed the look. End the magistrate. The Chairman's look will become a memory.

after_open_door_closed.name: After the Open Door Closed
after_open_door_closed.flavor: An open door in Mourncross closed behind you. A procession of one walks past it. End the one. The door will not need to close again.

rope_a_hear_the_bell.name: The Bellward's Rope — Hear the Bell
rope_a_hear_the_bell.flavor: Two of the procession ring the bell at first light. End them. The bell will fall silent for one morning.

rope_b_grasp_the_rope.name: The Bellward's Rope — Grasp the Rope
rope_b_grasp_the_rope.flavor: A magistrate guards the belfry door. End him. Take the rope. The Bellward will feel it leaving.

rope_c_close_the_belfry.name: The Bellward's Rope — Close the Belfry
rope_c_close_the_belfry.reward_chronicle_line: They climbed to the Mourncross belfry, took the Bellward's rope, and ended the Bellward herself with it in their other hand.
rope_c_close_the_belfry.flavor: The Bellward waits at the belfry. End her. The bell knows your name now. Close the belfry. It will not open again.

mourn_status_cleared.name: Walk Clean from the Hollow Procession
mourn_status_cleared.flavor: Three of the procession. When you walk out, no status clings to you. Mourncross measures who came through clean.

mourn_no_damage_magistrate.name: Untouched Through the Magistrate's Court
mourn_no_damage_magistrate.flavor: A magistrate. End him without a scratch. The capital's old guards did it this way to prove they were not afraid.

mourn_no_abilities_wretch.name: Three Wretches, Honest Steel
mourn_no_abilities_wretch.flavor: Three wretches. No ability used in any fight. Honest steel, the way the capital's first gutter-clearers worked.

hidden_window_face_count.name: A Face Was at the Window for One Count
hidden_window_face_count.flavor: Your first step in Mourncross — a face was at a window for exactly one count, then gone. A wretch saw it too. End it.

rangers_silent_streets.name: The Ranger's Silent Streets
rangers_silent_streets.flavor: Five of the procession, none of them aware. Only a ranger moves through Mourncross's streets that quietly.

after_old_seal_capital_less_hollow.name: After the Old Seal, the Capital Is Less Hollow
after_old_seal_capital_less_hollow.reward_chronicle_line: Once they had once made The Old Seal, Mourncross was less hollow by one count for them, and five of the procession ended easier.
after_old_seal_capital_less_hollow.flavor: Across runs, The Old Seal marks you. The procession is less hollow by one count for you. Five of them. End them easier.

after_choir_almost_sang_you.name: After the Choir Almost Sang You
after_choir_almost_sang_you.flavor: The Choir almost sang you into the rite. An effigy carries your half-sung name. End the effigy. The name will be yours.

after_lost_a_note.name: After You Lost a Note in the Choir
after_lost_a_note.flavor: A note of yours dropped in the Choir. Three censer-shades carry it in their smoke. End them. The note will return.

after_feather.name: After You Found a Feather in the Choir
after_feather.flavor: A feather is in your pack from the Choir. An effigy wants the feather as a relic. End the effigy. The feather stays.

after_left_thread.name: After You Left a Thread for Atrél
after_left_thread.flavor: A thread of yours waits on Atrél's altar. A golem holds the thread as evidence. End the golem. The thread is offered.

after_set_grief.name: After You Set a Grief at Atrél's Altar
after_set_grief.flavor: A grief of yours sits at Atrél's altar. Two censer-shades want to absorb it. End them. The grief stays where it is.

after_cannot_defend.name: After You Could Not Defend in the Choir
after_cannot_defend.reward_chronicle_line: Once they had been unable to defend in the Choir; they returned and ended the golem that had been there for the failed defense.
after_cannot_defend.flavor: Once you could not defend in the Choir. A golem stood there while the Choir struck. End the golem. The defense will hold next time.

after_altar_candle.name: After Atrél's Candle Did Not Flicker
after_altar_candle.flavor: Atrél's candle did not flicker for you. An effigy wants the candle's flame to fail. End the effigy. The flame holds.

choir_no_potions.name: No Potions in the Choir
choir_no_potions.flavor: An effigy. No potion crossed your lips while in the Choir. The Choir respects climbers who refuse the easy break.

choir_first_strike.name: First Strike Against the Censer
choir_first_strike.flavor: Two censer-shades. The first blow in each fight is yours. The Choir hates a first strike from outside.

choir_companion_kill_effigy.name: Let the Sworn Bring the Effigy Down
choir_companion_kill_effigy.flavor: An effigy. Your sworn lands the killing blow. The Choir hates sworn-kills more than first strikes.

choir_thrown_kill.name: Thrown Against the Censer
choir_thrown_kill.flavor: A censer-shade. Killed with a thrown weapon — no swung metal. The Choir's old wardens did it this way.

hidden_priest_recognized.name: A Priest at the Choir Recognized You
hidden_priest_recognized.flavor: Your first step in the Choir — a priest's effigy raised its head one count too sharply. End it. It recognized you.

mages_counter_pitch.name: The Mage's Counter-Pitch
mages_counter_pitch.reward_chronicle_line: A mage walked the Unanswered Choir and pitched three counter-songs against three effigies. The third line was hers, then.
mages_counter_pitch.flavor: Three effigies, sung against. Only a mage knows the pitch that makes the Choir's effigies sing wrong.

after_hidden_truth_choir.name: After the Hidden Truth, the Choir Already Knows
after_hidden_truth_choir.reward_chronicle_line: Once they had once known the hidden truth, Cantor Vael did not sing when they entered the Choir, and they took him in his silence.
after_hidden_truth_choir.flavor: Across runs, the Hidden Truth marks you. Cantor Vael does not sing when you enter. End him in the silence.

reborn_hollowed_at_altar.name: A Previous You Was a Hollowed at Atrél's Altar
reborn_hollowed_at_altar.reward_chronicle_line: A previous one of them had been a Hollowed kneeling at Atrél's altar in the Choir. The new self ended the effigy that had once stood next to them.
reborn_hollowed_at_altar.flavor: A previous you knelt at Atrél's altar as a Hollowed. One effigy stood there next to the previous you. End it.

after_relentless_taught_endure.name: After the Penitent's Relentless Taught You
after_relentless_taught_endure.flavor: The Penitent's relentless attack taught you to endure. Two ogres test what you learned. End them. The lesson was for use.

after_penitent_left_fang.name: After the Penitent Left You a Scavver Fang
after_penitent_left_fang.flavor: A scavver fang the Penitent set aside for you. Two wraiths want it back for the dead they were. End them. The fang is yours.

after_summit_wind_call.name: After the Summit Wind Called Your Step
after_summit_wind_call.flavor: The Summit wind called your step once. Three wraiths heard the call. End them. The wind will speak only to you.

after_fear_of_summit_stair.name: After You Knew Fear of the Summit Stair
after_fear_of_summit_stair.reward_chronicle_line: Once they had known fear of the Summit Stair, the stone-golem at its foot stepped aside; they took the golem in the stepping.
after_fear_of_summit_stair.flavor: You knew fear of the Summit Stair once. A golem keeps the stair. End him. The stair will be a stair after.

after_lamp_oil_for_climb.name: After the Lampkeeper Gave You Oil
after_lamp_oil_for_climb.flavor: The lampkeeper at Gravewatch gave you oil for the climb. A Penitent above the snowline burned the oil. End him. The lampkeeper will know.

after_a_child_promised_climber.name: After a Child Promised to Be a Climber Too
after_a_child_promised_climber.reward_chronicle_line: Once a child had promised them to be a climber too, the Penitent named the Summit for them, and they took the Penitent at the naming.
after_a_child_promised_climber.flavor: A child promised once to be a climber too. The Penitent wants the child's name. End him. The child will be safe.

after_breath_steamed_into_pall.name: After Your Breath Steamed Into the Pall
after_breath_steamed_into_pall.flavor: Your breath steamed into the Pall once. Four wraiths now carry the steam. End them. The Pall will exhale.

after_summit_almost_turned_back.name: After You Almost Turned Back
after_summit_almost_turned_back.reward_chronicle_line: Once they had almost turned back at the Summit step; they returned and ended the Penitent who had been ready to welcome the turning-back.
after_summit_almost_turned_back.flavor: You almost turned back. The Penitent at the upper stone was ready to welcome the turning. End him. You came back.

turning_a_step_outside.name: Turning Back — Step Outside the Climb
turning_a_step_outside.flavor: Two wraiths above the snowline argue. End them. The climb's old climbers turned back here. You will not, this time.

turning_b_walk_the_lower_path.name: Turning Back — Walk the Lower Path
turning_b_walk_the_lower_path.flavor: An ogre keeps the lower path. End him. The lower path is the path the climbers who turned back walked.

turning_c_face_the_penitent_who_let_you.name: Turning Back — Face the Penitent Who Let You
turning_c_face_the_penitent_who_let_you.reward_chronicle_line: They walked the climbers' turning-back path and ended the Penitent who had been letting them turn back. The climb went on after.
turning_c_face_the_penitent_who_let_you.flavor: The Penitent who lets climbers turn back is at the lower-path stone. End him. The turning-back will still be an option, for others.

climb_no_damage_wraith.name: Untouched Through the Wraith-Line
climb_no_damage_wraith.flavor: Two wraiths. No damage taken in either fight. The Climb's old wardens did it this way.

climb_thrown_kill_golem.name: Thrown Against the Stone-Golem
climb_thrown_kill_golem.flavor: A stone-golem, killed with a thrown weapon. The Climb's old climbers did it this way for the golems they had no quarrel with.

climb_low_hp_kill_ogre.name: An Ogre at the Last Hour
climb_low_hp_kill_ogre.flavor: An ogre, killed while you are at the last hour of your hp. The old climbers did this on purpose, to be sure they remembered.

hidden_summit_wind_first_step.name: The Summit Wind Marked Your First Step
hidden_summit_wind_first_step.flavor: The first step above the snowline — the Summit wind passed through you for one count. A wraith felt it. End the wraith. The wind will pass again.

after_warden_step_softer.name: After the Warden, the Step Is Softer
after_warden_step_softer.reward_chronicle_line: Once they had once become the Warden, the Summit ground was softer for them; two stone-golems fell easier under the new step.
after_warden_step_softer.flavor: Across runs, the Warden ending marks you. The Summit ground is softer for your step. Two stone-golems. They fall easier.

reborn_witherwood_younger_bandit.name: A Bandit a Previous You Could Not Take
reborn_witherwood_younger_bandit.flavor: A bandit your previous self could not take stands in the wood. The new you is older. Take him.
reborn_witherwood_younger_bandit.reward_chronicle_line: They returned for a bandit a previous one of them had fled from. The bandit fell to the older self.

reborn_witherwood_pack_lead.name: A Pack a Previous You Had Run From
reborn_witherwood_pack_lead.flavor: The pack lead at the Hunter's Cache once chased a previous you out. The new you walks back in.
reborn_witherwood_pack_lead.reward_chronicle_line: The Hunter's Cache pack lead remembered the previous self's scent, and ended at the new self's hand.

reborn_witherwood_giantbat.name: A Bat Your Previous Self Set Aside
reborn_witherwood_giantbat.flavor: A carrion bat the previous you nearly killed but spared. The new you finishes the work.

reborn_reach_silt_drowner.name: A Drowner Who Remembers the Previous You
reborn_reach_silt_drowner.flavor: A silt-drowner who almost took the previous you down. The new you is still standing. End it.

reborn_reach_thresher.name: A Thresher Standing on the Previous You's Field
reborn_reach_thresher.flavor: A thresher stands on the field the previous you tried to seed. End the thresher. The field will be remembered.
reborn_reach_thresher.reward_chronicle_line: A previous one of them had once tried to seed a Reach field; the thresher that stood there now stands no longer.

reborn_holds_red_beard_carved.name: A Red Beard Who Carved the Previous You's Name
reborn_holds_red_beard_carved.flavor: A Red Beard who had carved your previous self's name into the holds' doorpost. End him. The name stays carved.
reborn_holds_red_beard_carved.reward_chronicle_line: A Red Beard had carved a previous one of them into a doorpost. The new self returned and ended the carver.

reborn_holds_doorshut.name: A Doorshut Who Shut a Door on the Previous You
reborn_holds_doorshut.flavor: A doorshut who once shut a door on the previous you. End it. The door will open.

reborn_gullet_troll_carried_kettle.name: A Troll Who Took a Kettle the Previous You Carried
reborn_gullet_troll_carried_kettle.flavor: The kettle the previous you carried is in a troll's hand. End the troll. The kettle will be a kettle again.

reborn_gullet_slime_lost_word.name: A Slime Carrying a Word the Previous You Lost
reborn_gullet_slime_lost_word.flavor: A slime absorbed a word the previous you spoke into the Gullet. End it. The word will come back.

reborn_gullet_bat_button_pair.name: A Bat Carrying the Previous You's Button Pair
reborn_gullet_bat_button_pair.flavor: A bat carries the previous you's button pair on a thread. End the bat. The buttons stay paired.

reborn_mourn_magistrate_judged.name: A Magistrate Who Judged a Previous You
reborn_mourn_magistrate_judged.flavor: A magistrate had judged a previous you. End him. The judgment will be overturned.
reborn_mourn_magistrate_judged.reward_chronicle_line: A previous one of them had been judged by a Pall-Magistrate in Mourncross. The new self returned, and the verdict reversed.

reborn_mourn_procession_wore_clothes.name: A Procession Wearing a Previous You's Coat
reborn_mourn_procession_wore_clothes.flavor: One of the hollow procession wears a coat the previous you had owned. End them. The coat will be put away properly.

reborn_mourn_wretch_at_the_grave.name: A Wretch at a Previous You's Grave
reborn_mourn_wretch_at_the_grave.flavor: A wretch squats at the grave of a previous you. End it. The grave will be quiet.

reborn_choir_effigy_sang_for_you.name: An Effigy That Sang for a Previous You
reborn_choir_effigy_sang_for_you.flavor: An effigy in the Choir sang for the previous you's funeral. End the effigy. The funeral will be over.
reborn_choir_effigy_sang_for_you.reward_chronicle_line: An effigy had once sung at a previous one of them's funeral; the new self ended the singer.

reborn_choir_censer_carried_breath.name: A Censer Carrying a Previous You's Last Breath
reborn_choir_censer_carried_breath.flavor: A censer-shade carries the previous you's last breath in its smoke. End the shade. The breath will dissipate properly.

reborn_climb_penitent_handprint_above.name: A Penitent Whose Handprint Was Below the Previous You's
reborn_climb_penitent_handprint_above.flavor: A Penitent's handprint sits under the previous you's. He resents the ordering. End him. The handprints will settle.
reborn_climb_penitent_handprint_above.reward_chronicle_line: A Penitent had set his handprint below a previous one of them's. The new self ended the Penitent. The handprints stayed.

reborn_climb_wraith_was_climber.name: A Wraith Who Climbed With a Previous You
reborn_climb_wraith_was_climber.flavor: An ice-wraith was a climber once who climbed with a previous you. End the wraith. The climber will rest.

reborn_summit_warden_knew_old_step.name: The Summit Warden Knew the Previous You's Step
reborn_summit_warden_knew_old_step.flavor: The Summit Warden recognizes the previous you's step in the new you's stride. End the Warden. The step will be your own only.
reborn_summit_warden_knew_old_step.reward_chronicle_line: The Shadow Warden had recognized the previous one of them's step in the new self's stride; the new self ended the Warden, and the step was theirs alone.

warrior_mourn_gate.name: The Warrior's Gate at Mourncross
warrior_mourn_gate.flavor: Four magistrates at the capital's gate. A warrior holds the gate long enough that they all come, one by one. End them.
warrior_mourn_gate.reward_chronicle_line: A warrior held Mourncross's gate alone until four magistrates had fallen at it.

warrior_reach_line_hold.name: The Warrior's Line at the Reach
warrior_reach_line_hold.flavor: Three Lurchers come at the line a warrior holds at the Reach's edge. End them. The line will not break this winter.

warrior_summit_step.name: The Warrior's Step at the Summit
warrior_summit_step.flavor: The last narrow step before the Summit. A stone-golem keeps it. Only a warrior weighs enough to convince the step to hold both.

warrior_climb_wall_iii.name: The Warrior's Wall on the Climb
warrior_climb_wall_iii.flavor: Five ogres on the bare ridge. A warrior holds the wall against the wind and the ogres both.

rogue_witherwood_ambush.name: The Rogue's Ambush in the Wood
rogue_witherwood_ambush.flavor: Four bandits in a tight clearing. A rogue takes all four before any one notices the others falling.

rogue_choir_silent_steal.name: The Rogue's Silent Steal at the Choir
rogue_choir_silent_steal.flavor: A reliquary golem holds something a rogue is meant to take. End the golem in one strike. Take the thing. Leave with no sound.

rogue_holds_quiet_five.name: The Rogue's Quiet Five at the Holds
rogue_holds_quiet_five.flavor: Five doorshuts. Each one taken before the others know. The Holds' old guards lived this way once. A rogue carries the way.

mage_choir_altar_burn.name: The Mage's Altar-Burn at the Choir
mage_choir_altar_burn.flavor: Four censer-shades. A mage's counter-burn ends each one without letting any of them rejoin the smoke.

mage_summit_name_wind.name: The Mage's Name to the Summit Wind
mage_summit_name_wind.flavor: Three ice-wraiths. A mage speaks each one's name to the Summit wind as they fall. The wind will carry them.

mage_reach_quiet_song.name: The Mage's Quiet Song at the Reach
mage_reach_quiet_song.flavor: Four silt-drowners. A mage's quiet song breaks each of their harmonics. The Reach hears the breaking and is quieter.

ranger_climb_pathfinder.name: The Ranger's Pathfinder on the Climb
ranger_climb_pathfinder.flavor: A stone-golem holds the path. A ranger reads the path the climbers-before-the-climb made, and ends the golem on it.

ranger_witherwood_trail.name: The Ranger's Trail in the Wood
ranger_witherwood_trail.flavor: Six wolves. A ranger follows their pack-trail one by one, taking each without the others doubling back.

ranger_reach_dry_foot_ii.name: The Ranger's Dry Foot II
ranger_reach_dry_foot_ii.flavor: Four threshers, ended with a ranger's foot dry through every fight. Twice as hard as the first time. The Reach respects the doubling.

ranger_holds_tracker.name: The Ranger's Tracker at the Holds
ranger_holds_tracker.flavor: Three forager boys. A ranger tracks them through the holds' silent houses. Each one ends when the ranger chooses.
ranger_holds_tracker.reward_chronicle_line: A ranger tracked three forager boys through the Drowned Holds, the way the kingdom's old hunters had taught.

cleric_mourn_funeral.name: The Cleric's Funeral at Mourncross
cleric_mourn_funeral.flavor: Five of the procession. A cleric gives each one the funeral the kingdom owed them. They lay properly down after.
cleric_mourn_funeral.reward_chronicle_line: A cleric gave five of Mourncross's hollow procession the funeral the kingdom had never given.

cleric_choir_grief_lift.name: The Cleric's Grief-Lift at the Choir
cleric_choir_grief_lift.flavor: Two effigies. A cleric lifts the grief each one is meant to carry, and the effigy stops singing.

cleric_climb_last_rites.name: The Cleric's Last Rites on the Climb
cleric_climb_last_rites.flavor: Four ice-wraiths. A cleric gives last rites to each one. They rest after, the way they had been waiting to.

cleric_holds_bless_bonesongs.name: The Cleric's Blessing for the Bonesongs
cleric_holds_bless_bonesongs.flavor: Five bonesongs. A cleric blesses each one to silence. The Holds are quieter for it.

cc_no_stun_reach.name: No Stagger in the Reach
cc_no_stun_reach.flavor: Two threshers. Neither one knocks you off your footing. The Reach remembers your foot then.

cc_no_stun_climb.name: No Stagger Above the Snowline
cc_no_stun_climb.flavor: An ogre. The wind tries; the ogre tries; neither one staggers you.

cc_no_potions_reach.name: No Potions in the Reach
cc_no_potions_reach.flavor: Two lurchers. No potion crossed your lips while in the Reach. The Reach respects climbers who refuse the bottle.

cc_no_hireling_mourn.name: Your Sworn Survives the Capital
cc_no_hireling_mourn.flavor: Two magistrates. Your sworn stands at your side after each fight. The capital marks the keeping.

cc_companion_landed_kill_reach.name: The Sworn Closes the Reaper
cc_companion_landed_kill_reach.flavor: The Last Reaper. Your sworn lands the killing blow. The Reach's water doesn't answer.

cc_first_strike_mourn_processions.name: First Strike, Capital Processions
cc_first_strike_mourn_processions.flavor: Four of the procession. The first blow in each fight is yours. The Pall hears who struck first.

cc_one_round_dyke_warden.name: A Dyke-Warden in a Single Round
cc_one_round_dyke_warden.flavor: A dyke-warden ended in one round. The old water-keepers did it this way to spare the bystanders the noise.

cc_fled_then_returned_climb.name: Flee, Then Return — Above the Snowline
cc_fled_then_returned_climb.flavor: A Penitent. Flee the first encounter. Return. End him. The Climb respects the trick of leaving more than the trick of staying.

cc_no_damage_chairman.name: Untouched Against the Chairman
cc_no_damage_chairman.flavor: The Chairman. End him without a scratch on you. The sealed chamber will be sealed differently after.

cc_status_cleared_holds.name: Walk Clean from the Holds
cc_status_cleared_holds.flavor: Two doorshuts. When you walk out, no status clings to you. The Holds measure who came through clean.

cc_crit_killing_blow_climb.name: The Climber's Critical at the Step
cc_crit_killing_blow_climb.flavor: A stone-golem at the step. End it with a critical killing blow. The Climb's old grandmasters did it this way.

cc_full_stamina_choir.name: Walk the Choir With Full Breath
cc_full_stamina_choir.flavor: An effigy. End it without spending a single point of stamina. The Choir respects climbers who keep their breath whole.

cc_no_abilities_pallid_stag.name: Honest Steel Against the Stag
cc_no_abilities_pallid_stag.flavor: The Pallid Stag. Sword and feet only. The Witherwood's first hunters did it this way.

cc_thrown_lurcher.name: Thrown Against the Lurcher
cc_thrown_lurcher.flavor: A lurcher. Killed with a thrown weapon — no swung metal. The Reach's old hunters did it this way for the lurchers they had no quarrel with.

cc_pet_assist_silt.name: Let the Pet Bring the Silt-Drowners Down II
cc_pet_assist_silt.flavor: Three silt-drowners. Your pet lands the drop-below-half blow on each. The Reach respects the pet's footwork.

cc_low_hp_devoured.name: An Honest Fight at the Last Hour
cc_low_hp_devoured.flavor: The Devoured Captain ended while you are at the last hour of your hp. The old captains did it this way for the captains they had loved once.

cc_dodging_then_killing_gullet.name: Dodge, Then Strike
cc_dodging_then_killing_gullet.flavor: Two cave-trolls. Each killing blow lands on a turn after you dodged. The kingdom's old rangers did it this way.

cc_stun_killing_blow_bandit.name: End the Bandits While Stunned
cc_stun_killing_blow_bandit.flavor: Three bandits. Each killing blow lands while the bandit is stunned. The kingdom's old patrolmen did it this way.

cc_no_healing_red_beard.name: Refuse Healing in the Holds
cc_no_healing_red_beard.flavor: A Red Beard. No healing received — no potion, no companion, nothing. The Holds' old guards did it this way for the bargain.

cc_unarmed_kill_doorshut.name: Unarmed Against the Doorshut
cc_unarmed_kill_doorshut.flavor: A doorshut. End it with no weapon equipped. The Holds' old fighters did it this way for the doorshuts they had loved once.

cc_named_at_death_giant_bat.name: Name the Bats at Death
cc_named_at_death_giant_bat.flavor: Four giant bats. Speak each one's true name as the killing blow lands. The wood's old hunters knew the names.
